en: "Judge not, that you be not judged.
zh: “不可判斷人，免得你們被判斷。

en: For with the judgment you pronounce you will be judged, and with the measure you use it will be measured to you.
zh: 你們怎樣判斷人，也必怎樣被判斷；你們用甚麼標準衡量人，也必照樣被衡量。

en: Why do you see the speck that is in your brother's eye, but do not notice the log that is in your own eye?
zh: 為甚麼看見你弟兄眼中的木屑，卻不理會自己眼中的梁木呢？

en: Or how can you say to your brother, 'Let me take the speck out of your eye,' when there is the log in your own eye?
zh: 你自己眼中有梁木，怎能對弟兄說：‘讓我除掉你眼中的木屑’呢？

en: You hypocrite, first take the log out of your own eye, and then you will see clearly to take the speck out of your brother's eye.
zh: 偽君子啊！先除掉你眼中的梁木，才可以看得清楚，去除掉弟兄眼中的木屑。

en: "Do not give dogs what is holy, and do not throw your pearls before pigs, lest they trample them underfoot and turn to attack you.
zh: 不要把聖物給狗，也不要把你們的珍珠丟在豬前，免得牠們用腳把珍珠踐踏，又轉過來猛噬你們。 祈求就必得著

en: "Ask, and it will be given to you; seek, and you will find; knock, and it will be opened to you.
zh: “你們祈求，就給你們；尋找，就尋見；叩門，就給你們開門。

en: For everyone who asks receives, and the one who seeks finds, and to the one who knocks it will be opened.
zh: 因為凡祈求的就得著，尋找的就尋見，叩門的就給他開門。

en: Or which one of you, if his son asks him for bread, will give him a stone?
zh: 你們中間哪一個人，兒子向他要餅，反給他石頭；

en: Or if he asks for a fish, will give him a serpent?
zh: 要魚，反給他蛇呢？

en: If you then, who are evil, know how to give good gifts to your children, how much more will your Father who is in heaven give good things to those who ask him!
zh: 你們雖然邪惡，尚且知道把好東西給兒女，何況你們在天上的父，難道不更把好東西賜給求他的人嗎？

en: "So whatever you wish that others would do to you, do also to them, for this is the Law and the Prophets.
zh: 所以，你們願意人怎樣待你們，你們也要怎樣待人，這是律法和先知的總綱。

en: "Enter by the narrow gate. For the gate is wide and the way is easy that leads to destruction, and those who enter by it are many.
zh: “你們當進窄門，因為引到滅亡的門是寬的，路是大的，進去的人也多；

en: For the gate is narrow and the way is hard that leads to life, and those who find it are few.
zh: 但引到生命的門是窄的，路是小的，找著的人也少。

en: "Beware of false prophets, who come to you in sheep's clothing but inwardly are ravenous wolves.
zh: “提防假先知！他們披著羊皮到你們當中，裡面卻是殘暴的狼。

en: You will recognize them by their fruits. Are grapes gathered from thornbushes, or figs from thistles?
zh: 憑著他們的果子就可以認出他們來：荊棘裡怎能摘到葡萄？蒺藜裡怎能摘到無花果呢？

en: So, every healthy tree bears good fruit, but the diseased tree bears bad fruit.
zh: 照樣，好樹結好果子，壞樹結壞果子；

en: A healthy tree cannot bear bad fruit, nor can a diseased tree bear good fruit.
zh: 好樹不能結壞果子，壞樹也不能結好果子。

en: Every tree that does not bear good fruit is cut down and thrown into the fire.
zh: 凡是不結好果子的樹，就被砍下來，丟在火中。

en: Thus you will recognize them by their fruits.
zh: 因此你們憑著他們的果子就可以認出他們來。

en: "Not everyone who says to me, 'Lord, Lord,' will enter the kingdom of heaven, but the one who does the will of my Father who is in heaven.
zh: “不是每一個對我說：‘主啊，主啊！’的人，都能進入天國，唯有遵行我天父旨意的人，才能進去。

en: On that day many will say to me, 'Lord, Lord, did we not prophesy in your name, and cast out demons in your name, and do many mighty works in your name?'
zh: 到那日，必有許多人對我說：‘主啊，主啊！難道我們沒有奉你的名講道，奉你的名趕鬼，奉你的名行過許多神蹟嗎？’

en: And then will I declare to them, 'I never knew you; depart from me, you workers of lawlessness.'
zh: 但我必向他們聲明：‘我從來不認識你們；你們這些作惡的人，離開我去吧！’

en: "Everyone then who hears these words of mine and does them will be like a wise man who built his house on the rock.
zh: “所以，凡聽見我這些話又遵行的，就像聰明的人，把自己的房子蓋在磐石上。

en: And the rain fell, and the floods came, and the winds blew and beat on that house, but it did not fall, because it had been founded on the rock.
zh: 雨淋、水沖、風吹，搖撼那房子，房子卻不倒塌，因為建基在磐石上。

en: And everyone who hears these words of mine and does not do them will be like a foolish man who built his house on the sand.
zh: 凡聽見我這些話卻不遵行的，就像愚蠢的人，把自己的房子蓋在沙土上。

en: And the rain fell, and the floods came, and the winds blew and beat against that house, and it fell, and great was the fall of it."
zh: 雨淋、水沖、風吹，搖撼那房子，房子就倒塌了，並且倒塌得很厲害。”

en: And when Jesus finished these sayings, the crowds were astonished at his teaching,
zh: 耶穌講完了這些話，群眾都驚奇他的教訓。

en: for he was teaching them as one who had authority, and not as their scribes.
zh: 因為耶穌教導他們，像一個有權柄的人，不像他們的經學家。
